compatibility_report:
Street Fighter 6 (1364780) Crashes while loading matches

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6970.
@malucious81 posted on 2023-07-27T03:49:24:

# Compatibility Report
- Street Fighter 6
- Steam AppID: 1364780

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6700XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.1.3
- Kernel version: 6.4.4-200
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/malucious81/032087799e309a0004988c04e7d9b7d8)
- Proton version: 8.0-3, Experimental, GE-8-9
- Journald during crash: [Gist](https://gist.github.com/malucious81/9403832f3e2e6423e13c043a8606ead4)

## Symptoms 
Street fighter 6 is crashing when entering matches, which for me also ends up crashing my entire system. 
To describe the crash, the display goes completely black and I can still hear audio from the game. Eventually the audio stops and the system becomes unresponsive, no keyboard or mouse inputs.
I end up having to do a manual reset.

## Reproduction
- Start Street Fighter 6 and enter the Fighting Ground.
- Choose Versus 1 on 1 match.
- Select your Character or Characters and start the match.
- The game reaches the screen where the fighters can make faces at each other, but when it fades to black, the game crashes instead of loading the fighting stage.
- This crash occurs when attempting to enter an online match as well.

## Notes
- This crash does not happen EVERY time, but it never happened until the recent Street Fighter 6 update.
- I was SSH'd into the gaming system during the crash but I was still unable to issue a reboot command.

[steam-1364780.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12179023/steam-1364780.log)

**I see multiple issues relative to EAC, this comment is different: I applied the solution to rename `eldenring.exe` to `start_protected_game.exe`. When clicking "play", the game starts full-screen for a second then crashes (black screen with game cursor only). [Other people seem to have a similar issue](https://old.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/t10fwz/er_white_screen_crash/)**

**EDIT**: I just had a proton experimental (bleeding edge) update, now I get a white screen.

**EDIT 2**: Switch from `KDE Plasma (X.org)` to `KDE Plasma (Wayland)` fixed my issue.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: ELDEN RING
- Steam AppID of the game: 1245620

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050/PCIe/SSE2
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 510.54
- Kernel version: 5.16.11-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): <https://gist.github.com/demurgos/99e5de47d691a20aeff208189711dd96>
- Proton version: Proton Experimental (bleeding edge) (also tested with Proton-7.2-GE-2)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

[Proton log](https://gist.github.com/demurgos/cd3b9f4a51ba0d346271db8197a85402)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

I see multiple issues relative to EAC, this comment is different: I applied the solution to rename `eldenring.exe` to `start_protected_game.exe`. When clicking "play", the game starts full-screen for a second then crashes (black screen with game cursor only). [Other people seem to have a similar issue](https://old.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/t10fwz/er_white_screen_crash/)

**EDIT**: I just had a proton experimental (bleeding edge) update, now I get a white screen.

**EDIT 2**: Switch from `KDE Plasma (X.org)` to `KDE Plasma (Wayland)` fixed my issue.

## Reproduction

1. Opt into the bleeding edge version of Proton experimental
2. Rename `eldenring.exe` to `start_protected_game.exe`.
3. Click "Play"
4. The game starts for a second (black screen with game cursor) then crashes.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The issue I have with this game is that there is alot of mouse input lag / delay, to the point where the game is not enjoyable anymore, and I only have this mouse input lag issue on this game.

It also varies on different distros, for example on Linux mint on X11 there seems to be less mouse input lag on this game, but I can still feel it's not as smooth as it should be, but on cachyos on wayland the mouse input lag is extremely worse.

I've tested with Proton Experimental bleeding edge, hotfix and 9.0-4 and they all have the same issue.

## Reproduction

Open the game

Move the mouse cursor

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:  Monster Rancher 1 & 2 DX
- Steam AppID of the game:  1716120

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070/PCIe/SSE2
- Driver/LLVM version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 470.161.03
- Kernel version: 5.15.0-69
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):  https://gist.github.com/robotjunkyard/fab743e7f665c5d96d641269c8a999f9
- Proton version:  8.0-1

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

This report pertains to the Launcher that starts up, not the games themselves.

Using "Proton 8.0-1" compatibility and launching the game, going into the Keyboard & Video Resolution options window from the launcher pops up an error and fails out, dropping back to the Launcher:
> "Initialization of DirectX failed.  A device could not be found (-2113929215)"

This options window is the only known way to toggle Fullscreen mode, video resolution, and change controller/input settings.

## Reproduction

Launch game using Proton 8.0-1
In launcher:
- Choose Options
- Choose Keyboard/Resolution Settings
- That will launch what seems to be another program titled [b]"MonsterRancher 1&2 DX" Startup Settings[/b]
- You will see the error message about DirectX
- Options window then shuts down and drops back to the launcher

## Workaround
Reverting back to "Proton 7.0-6" resolves this specific issue.

## Proton debug log file
Link: [steam-1716120.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/11355579/steam-1716120.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Steam overlay is not opening.

## Reproduction

- start game 
- try to buy Bonds (premium currency) by clicking on them
- loading indicator is shown but no redirect is happening to the purchasing steam overlay
-  sometimes game freezes but most of the time you just can proceed without nothing ever happend

## Additional Information
Steam overlay is enabled globally and for the specific game. It works on the Steam Deck.

[steam-1313140.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/11368717/steam-1313140.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
After playing the game for a relatively short amount of time, the game freezes and starts having audio played in a loop.
What's worse, the entire system freezes, requiring a hard system reboot.
Around the time it occurs, I imagine this access violation error is thrown in the log:

## Reproduction

1. Launch the game
2. Play the game for about ~30 minutes or less
(when it specifically happens is unknown but it doesn't take long to trigger in normal gameplay)
(Usually happens at the player's camp, from what I've experienced)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: World of Warships
- Steam AppID of the game: 552990

Your system information
https://gist.github.com/demib72/2f915c3c97a29f3d722006f6dab0b173

Tried Proton Experimental, 6.3-8, 5.13-6, 3.16-9

Please describe your issue in as much detail as possible:
I recently reinstalled World of Warships and got it to work once. Afterwards, the game would start but fail to properly render the game and now it will not start at all. It might be related to ValveSoftware/Proton#5340, but one of the fixes like no_fsync option has made any difference for me.

Proton log: https://gist.github.com/demib72/bed3f92807e1a24cdeac80acc192ca3b

1. Run World of Warships from Steam
